title: TC001-Enter docs from homepage via primary navigation
description: Verifies a first-time visitor can scroll the homepage, see component preview cards rendered, and enter the docs landing page via primary navigation.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/")
        
        # -> Scroll the page down to reveal the marketing sections and the embedded component preview cards below the hero.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/header/div/div/nav/a').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Return to the homepage (click the site logo) and scroll to the marketing/component preview section to verify preview cards are visible.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/header/div/div/a').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Click the primary navigation 'Docs' link to open the documentation landing page and verify the docs page loads.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/header/div/div/nav/a').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC003-Deep-link to theming docs and return to homepage via header
description: Verifies a developer can open the theming doc directly, see MDX rendered with sidebar context, then return to the homepage via header navigation and re-enter docs.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/")
        
        # -> Navigate to /docs/theming (http://localhost:3000/docs/theming) and load the documentation page so we can verify MDX rendering and the sidebar.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/docs/theming")
        
        # -> Click the header logo to go to the homepage, then click the header 'Docs' link to re-enter the docs index.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/header/div/div/a').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/header/div/div/nav/a').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Click the header logo (index 4533) to navigate to the homepage.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/header/div/div/a').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Click the header 'Docs' link to re-enter the documentation index and verify the docs page loads.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/header/div/div/nav/a').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC007-Copy-page control produces non-empty copied content
description: Verifies a developer can use the copy-page control on a representative doc page and the copied result is non-empty when pasted into an in-page field.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/")
        
        # -> Open the Docs page so I can navigate to the Installation doc and look for a copy-page control.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/header/div/div/nav/a').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Open the Installation doc page so we can use the page 'Copy Page' control there and paste into an in-page input.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/div/div/div/div/div[2]/div[2]/ul/li[2]/a').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Click the 'Copy Page' control to copy the page content, open the search input to receive paste, and paste clipboard contents into that input.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/div/div/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/div/div/div/div/div/div/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/header/div/div/div/div/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Focus the visible search input, paste clipboard contents (Ctrl+V), wait for UI to update, then extract the visible input content to verify it is non-empty.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[4]/div[2]/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC011-Per-page MDX markdown route returns raw markdown for docs
description: Verifies the /llms.mdx/docs/.../content.md route serves processed markdown (text/markdown) for a representative docs page, matching the MDX-backed docs content.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/")
        
        # -> Navigate to /llms.mdx/docs/installation/content.md, fetch the visible response body and confirm it contains a markdown heading and is non-empty; then open the HTML docs route for the same slug and compare the page title/topic.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/llms.mdx/docs/installation/content.md")
        
        # -> Open the HTML docs route for the same slug (http://localhost:3000/llms.mdx/docs/installation), capture the visible page title/topic, and compare it to the markdown heading.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/llms.mdx/docs/installation")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., '# Installation')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The response should be served as raw markdown so the page body should include the markdown heading '# Installation'.","assert (await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., '# Installation')]").nth(0).text_content()) != "", "The markdown response body should be non-empty and include a markdown heading.","assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Installation')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The HTML docs page should show the same page title 'Installation' as the markdown heading."]}
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

The markdown content route exists and returns content, but it does not match the rendered HTML docs page for the same slug.

Observations:
- The raw markdown route /llms.mdx/docs/installation/content.md contains the heading '# Installation'.
- The HTML docs page at /llms.mdx/docs/installation displays the heading '# Introduction'.
- The two pages' topics/headings differ, so the processed markdown does not align with the HTML docs page for this slug.

title: TC012-Terminal theme selector updates the in-browser terminal renderer
description: Verifies changing the terminal theme in the docs preview chrome updates the xterm/Ink preview (e.g. background/foreground tokens applied via terminalThemeKey), not only the select label.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/")
        
        # -> Click the 'Docs' link to open the documentation index, then navigate to the theming page (/docs/theming) and locate the terminal theme dropdown in the preview chrome.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/header/div/div/nav/a').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Click the 'Theming' link in the left sidebar to open /docs/theming and reveal the terminal preview with the terminal theme dropdown.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/div/div/div/div/div[2]/div[2]/ul/li[5]/a').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Extract the current computed background/foreground color of the terminal preview (xterm canvas or .terminal-preview-root) and the select's current label, then click the terminal theme NativeSelect to open its options.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/div/div/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/div[2]/div/div/div[3]/div/select').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC013-Search: Meta+C copies install command and Enter/click navigates
description: Verifies the command menu highlights a registry item; ⌘+C / Ctrl+C copies the shadcn add command. There is no in-UI copy confirmation (the dialog closes on copy)—assert success via navigator.clipboard.readText() after the shortcut. Separately, open search again and select the same result (Enter or click) and assert navigation to the component docs URL.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/")
        
        # -> Open the documentation search dialog (click the 'Search documentation...' control).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/header/div/div/div/div/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Type 'badge' in the search input, highlight/select the Badge registry result, copy via Ctrl/C (or Cmd/C if necessary), read the clipboard, reopen search, activate the Badge result, and verify the URL path.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[4]/div[2]/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('badge')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[4]/div[2]/div[2]/div/div[4]/div[2]/div').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        clipboard_text = await frame.evaluate("() => navigator.clipboard.readText()")
        assert 'shadcn add' in clipboard_text, "The clipboard should contain the shadcn add install command with the registry slug after copying from the command menu"
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert '/docs/components/badge' in current_url, "The page should have navigated to /docs/components/badge after activating the Badge search result"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC014-Analytics events fire for tracked copy actions
description: Verifies Vercel Analytics receives custom track() events when copying the install command from search. Intercept POST `/_vercel/insights/event` (application/json). Payload uses `en` for the event name (`copy_npm_command`) and `ed.code` for the full install line (e.g. `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @termcn/...`), plus `o`, `sdkn`, `sdkv`, `ts`, etc.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert len(captured_insight_events) > 0, "At least one POST to /_vercel/insights/event should have been sent after copying the install command"
        assert captured_insight_events[0].get('en') == 'copy_npm_command' and isinstance(captured_insight_events[0].get('ed', {}).get('code'), str) and captured_insight_events[0]['ed']['code'].strip() != '' and 'shadcn' in captured_insight_events[0]['ed']['code'], "The analytics payload should have en set to 'copy_npm_command' and ed.code should be a non-empty install command string containing 'shadcn' after copying the install command"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
